cases.title: [Cookie Banner] Verify that the Content section renders the correct drop-downs when GDPR is selected
cases.preconditions: User is on Cookie Banner > Content tab. GDPR is selected in the law selector (this is the default). The Content section shows a Consent Template card at the top. Below the card, five collapsible accordions are visible in collapsed state.

steps.content: Observe the law selector in the Consent Template card.
steps.expected: The law selector should show "GDPR" as the selected value by default.

steps.content: Observe the list of accordion options below the Consent Template card.
steps.expected: The following five accordions should be visible and collapsed by default: Cookie Notice, Preference Centre, Cookie List, Revisit Consent Button, Blocked Content.

cases.rewrite_notes: Stripped [Account Owner][Admin][Editor][Content][GDPR] prefixes. Collapsed navigation steps (Go to C205, navigate to Cookie Banner, verify sidebar sections, navigate to Content) into precondition. Dropped 'Consent Template card displayed at top' position step — static layout check. Kept law selector default state and accordion list as structural presence checks. All 12 role+plan variants collapsed (3 roles × 4 plan sections).

cases.title: [Cookie Banner] Verify that the Cookie Notice drop-down reveals all fields and premium indicators
cases.preconditions: User is on Cookie Banner > Content tab. GDPR is selected (default). The Cookie Notice accordion is collapsed.

steps.content: Click the "Cookie Notice" accordion to expand it.
steps.expected: The following items should be revealed: Title text field, Message text field (rich text editor), Close [X] button toggle, "Accept All" button text field, "Reject All" button toggle with text field, "Customise" button toggle with text field, "Cookie Policy" link toggle with URL text field, Custom logo text field, Disable CookieYes branding toggle. The "Custom logo" and "Disable CookieYes branding" controls should each display an upgrade icon (orange crown).

steps.content: Observe the premium icons on "Custom logo" and "Disable CookieYes branding".
steps.expected: Both controls should be locked and display an upgrade icon (orange crown), confirming they require an Ultimate plan upgrade to use.

cases.rewrite_notes: Stripped prefixes. Navigation steps collapsed into precondition. Tooltip copy verification step dropped (static copy). Premium icon presence is kept — it signals an access-gating logic state.

cases.title: [Cookie Banner] Verify that Cookie Notice text fields accept edits and reflect changes in the banner preview
cases.preconditions: User is on Cookie Banner > Content tab. GDPR is selected (default). User is on an Ultimate plan (Custom logo and Disable CookieYes branding are unlocked). Cookie Notice accordion is expanded.

steps.expected: Pre-filled with "We value your privacy". The text should appear on the banner first layer preview.

steps.content: Clear the Title field and type new text.
steps.expected: The updated text should appear on the banner first layer preview in real time.

Pre-filled with the default privacy message ("We use cookies to enhance your browsing experience, serve personalised ads or content, and analyse our traffic. By clicking 'Accept All', you consent to our use of cookies."). The text should appear on the banner first layer preview.
The updated message should appear on the banner first layer preview in real time.
Pre-filled with "Accept All". The button should appear on the banner first layer preview.
Updated label should appear on the banner first layer preview in real time.

steps.content: Observe the "Reject All" button text field (with Reject All toggle enabled).
steps.expected: Pre-filled with "Reject All". The button should appear on the banner first layer preview.
Updated label should appear in real time.

steps.content: Observe the "Customize" button text field (with Customize toggle enabled).
steps.expected: Pre-filled with "Customise". The button should appear on the banner first layer preview.
Updated label should appear in real time.

steps.content: Observe the "Cookie Policy" link text field (with Cookie Policy toggle enabled).
steps.expected: Pre-filled with "Cookie Policy".

steps.content: Edit the Cookie Policy link text.
steps.expected: Updated label should be editable.
Empty by default, with placeholder "URL of your Cookie Policy".
Pre-filled with "#". Entering a valid image URL should display the custom logo on the banner first layer preview.
All changes should be saved and reflected on the live website.

cases.rewrite_notes: Stripped prefixes. Navigation and sidebar verification steps collapsed into precondition. Original case begins with a full Stripe upgrade flow (multiple steps) — collapsed to 'User is on an Ultimate plan.' Tooltip copy steps dropped. Custom logo field kept here as part of the text fields group.

cases.title: [Cookie Banner] Verify that the Message field is disabled and replaced with IAB TCF text when "Support IAB TCF v2.3" is enabled
cases.preconditions: User is on Cookie Banner > Content tab. GDPR is selected (default). The "Support IAB TCF v2.3" toggle has been enabled in the General section. Cookie Notice accordion is expanded. The Message text field is visible.

steps.content: Observe the Message text field with IAB TCF v2.3 enabled.
steps.expected: The Message field should be disabled (non-editable) and pre-filled with the IAB TCF-approved consent text ("We and our {{count}} partners use cookies and other tracking technologies to improve your experience on our website..."). The field is a rich text editor (Quill) rendered read-only.

steps.content: Attempt to click inside the Message field and type.
steps.expected: The field should not accept input — it should remain non-editable.

cases.rewrite_notes: Stripped prefixes. Navigation steps collapsed into precondition. Original begins with Stripe upgrade flow — collapsed. Tooltip copy step dropped. Core behavior (disabled state and IAB text substitution) preserved.

cases.title: [Cookie Banner] Verify that Cookie Notice toggles control button and link visibility on the banner
cases.preconditions: User is on Cookie Banner > Content tab. GDPR is selected (default). User is on an Ultimate plan. Cookie Notice accordion is expanded.

steps.expected: The toggle should be disabled (off) by default. No close button should be visible on the banner first layer preview.
An "X" close button should appear in the top-left corner of the banner first layer preview.
The toggle should be enabled (on) by default. The "Reject All" button should be visible on the banner first layer preview.
The "Reject All" button should disappear from the banner first layer preview.
The toggle should be enabled (on) by default. The "Customise" button should be visible on the banner first layer preview.
The "Customise" button should disappear from the banner first layer preview.
The toggle should be disabled (off) by default. No Cookie Policy link should appear on the banner preview.

steps.content: Enable the "Cookie Policy" link toggle and enter a valid URL in the URL field.
steps.expected: A "Cookie Policy" link should appear on the banner first layer preview. Clicking it should open the cookie policy page in a new tab.
The toggle should be disabled (off) by default. "Powered by CookieYes" branding should be visible on the banner second layer.
The "Powered by CookieYes" branding should be removed from the banner second layer preview.
All toggle changes should be saved and reflected on the live website.

cases.rewrite_notes: Stripped prefixes. Original starts with Stripe upgrade flow — collapsed. Tooltip copy step dropped. C15038 ('Disable CookieYes branding icon for Ultimate users' [GDPR]) folded into this case as step 10 — tests the same toggle behavior under the same precondition.

cases.title: [Cookie Banner] Verify that enabling the "Show Google Privacy Policy" toggle reveals editable link fields in the Preference Center
cases.preconditions: User is on Cookie Banner > Content tab. GDPR is selected (default). Preference Center accordion is expanded. The "Show Google Privacy Policy" toggle is visible and disabled by default.

steps.content: Observe the "Show Google Privacy Policy" toggle.
steps.expected: The toggle should be disabled (off) by default.

steps.content: Enable the "Show Google Privacy Policy" toggle.
steps.expected: Three new fields should appear: Message text field, Link text text field, URL text field.

Pre-filled with "For more information on how Google's third-party cookies operate and handle your data, see:". This message with the Google Privacy Policy link should appear below the privacy overview on the banner second layer preview.
Updated text should appear on the banner second layer preview in real time.
Pre-filled with "Google Privacy Policy".
Updated link label should appear on the banner second layer preview in real time.
Pre-filled with "https://business.safety.google/privacy".

steps.content: Edit the URL field with a different URL, then click the link in the banner preview.
steps.expected: The updated URL should open in a new tab.
Changes should be saved. The Google Privacy Policy link should appear on the live website's banner second layer.

cases.rewrite_notes: Stripped prefixes. Original starts with Stripe upgrade flow — collapsed. Tooltip copy steps dropped.

cases.title: [Cookie Banner] Verify that the Embed code in Cookie List can be copied and embeds the cookie audit table on a website
cases.preconditions: User is on Cookie Banner > Content tab. GDPR is selected (default). Cookie List accordion is expanded. Tester has access to the target website's admin panel to paste HTML.

steps.expected:
A text area should display the HTML snippet `
` and a Copy button should appear above it.
The HTML code should be copied to the clipboard. The "Copy" button label should change to "Copied!".

steps.content: Paste the copied code inside the `<body>` tag of the target website page and save.
steps.expected: The code should be saved successfully.

steps.content: Navigate to the edited page on the live website.
steps.expected: A cookie audit table should be displayed on the page, listing all cookies grouped by category.

cases.rewrite_notes: Stripped prefixes. Navigation steps collapsed into precondition. Tooltip copy step dropped. C979 has no law prefix — this case applies to both GDPR and US State Laws. Requires live website access — not fully automatable via Playwright.

steps.expected: An "Alt text for blocked content" label with a help icon and a corresponding text field should be displayed.
Pre-filled with "Please accept cookies to access this content".
The field should accept the new text.
The edited alt text should be displayed on embedded content (e.g. YouTube iframes) on the live website before the user has given consent.

cases.title: [Cookie Banner] Verify that the upgrade tooltip on the Custom logo premium icon navigates to the Plans page
cases.preconditions: User is on any plan below Ultimate. User is on Cookie Banner > Content tab. Cookie Notice accordion is expanded. The Custom logo field displays an upgrade icon (orange crown).

steps.content: Click the upgrade icon (orange crown) on the Custom logo field.
steps.expected: An upgrade tooltip should appear with the headline 'Make your banner uniquely yours with a custom brand logo', body text 'Personalise your cookie banner with your brand logo for a seamless user experience.', 'Available in: Ultimate plan' label, and a single 'Upgrade now' button. (Note: 'Try Pro for Free' does not appear — this feature requires Ultimate, not Pro.)
User should be navigated to the Plans/payment page showing the upgrade path to Ultimate.

cases.rewrite_notes: Plan gate nudge case — /migrate-section should route this to 11. Billing & Upgrade > Plan Gates. GRILL FIX: 'Try Pro for Free' button does not appear for Ultimate-gated features on any plan tier. Tooltip only shows 'Upgrade now'. Merged Free and Pro scenarios — behavior is identical. Original source cases (C14989, C14995, C15001) may have been written when this feature was Pro-gated.

cases.title: [Cookie Banner] Verify that enabling the "Respect Global Privacy Control" toggle honors GPC browser signals and displays a notice on the banner
cases.preconditions: User is on Cookie Banner > Content tab. Law selector is set to US State Laws. User is on a Pro or higher plan. Opt-out Center accordion is expanded.

steps.content: Observe the "Respect Global Privacy Control" toggle.
steps.expected: The toggle should be disabled (off) by default. The Global Privacy Control notice text field should also be disabled.

steps.content: Observe the Global Privacy Control notice text field.
steps.expected: Pre-filled with "Your opt-out settings for this website have been respected since we detected a Global Privacy Control signal from your browser and, therefore, you cannot change this setting.".

steps.content: Enable the "Respect Global Privacy Control" toggle.
steps.expected: The toggle should turn on. The Global Privacy Control notice text field should become enabled.

Changes should be saved. On the live website, visitors whose browser sends a GPC signal should see the "Do Not Sell or Share My Personal Information" checkbox pre-checked and the GPC notice text displayed below it on the banner second layer.

steps.content: Edit the Global Privacy Control notice text field and click "Publish Changes".
steps.expected: The updated notice text should appear on the banner second layer for GPC users.

cases.rewrite_notes: Stripped prefixes. Original starts with Stripe upgrade flow — collapsed. Tooltip copy step dropped. Core behavior (toggle enables GPC signal honoring, notice text becomes editable) preserved.

User should be navigated to the Plans page showing the upgrade path to Ultimate.

cases.rewrite_notes: Plan gate nudge case — /migrate-section should route this to 11. Billing & Upgrade > Plan Gates.

cases.title: [Cookie Banner] Verify that the "Floating button" toggle controls whether the revisit consent widget appears on the website
cases.preconditions: User is on Cookie Banner > Content tab. Revisit Consent Button accordion is expanded. The Floating button toggle is enabled by default.

steps.expected: The toggle should be enabled (on) by default.

steps.content: Navigate to the live website and accept cookie consent on the banner.
steps.expected: A floating consent revisit button should appear in the bottom-left corner of the website.

steps.content: Click the floating button on the website.
steps.expected: The banner second layer (Preference Center or Opt-out Center, depending on law setting) should be displayed, allowing the user to update consent preferences.

steps.content: Return to Cookie Banner > Content > Revisit Consent Button in the app. Disable the "Floating button" toggle.
steps.expected: The toggle should turn off.
The floating consent revisit button should no longer appear on the website after consent is granted.

cases.rewrite_notes: Stripped prefixes. Navigation steps collapsed into precondition. Original expected result on the disable step was 'toggle should be displayed' — this is a copy error in the source; rewritten to 'toggle should turn off.'

cases.title: [Cookie Banner] Verify that the Position option controls the placement of the floating revisit consent button
cases.preconditions: User is on Cookie Banner > Content tab. Revisit Consent Button accordion is expanded. The Floating button toggle is enabled.

steps.expected: Two options should be available: "Left" and "Right". "Left" should be selected by default.

steps.content: Navigate to the live website and accept cookie consent.
steps.expected: The floating button should appear in the bottom-left corner.

steps.content: Return to Cookie Banner > Content > Revisit Consent Button. Select "Right" under Position.
steps.expected: "Right" should become the selected position option.
The floating button should now appear in the bottom-right corner on the website when consent is granted.

cases.rewrite_notes: Stripped prefixes. Navigation steps collapsed into precondition.

cases.title: [Cookie Banner] Verify that Revisit Consent Button text fields accept edits and reflect changes on the website
cases.preconditions: User is on Cookie Banner > Content tab. User is on an Ultimate plan (Custom icon is unlocked). Revisit Consent Button accordion is expanded.

steps.content: Observe the Custom icon text field.
steps.expected: Pre-filled with "#" (placeholder).

steps.content: Enter a valid image URL in the Custom icon field.
steps.expected: The custom image should replace the default revisit consent button icon on the website after publishing.

steps.content: Observe the "Text on hover" text field.
steps.expected: Pre-filled with "Consent Preferences". This text appears as a tooltip on hover over the floating button on the website.
The new text should be accepted.
The custom icon and updated hover text should appear on the floating button on the live website.

cases.rewrite_notes: Stripped prefixes. Original begins with Stripe upgrade flow — collapsed to precondition 'User is on an Ultimate plan.'

cases.title: [Cookie Banner] Verify that the 'GDPR & US State Laws' consent template option is Pro-gated
cases.preconditions: User is logged in on any plan below Pro (Free or Basic). User is on Cookie Banner > Content tab. The Consent Template dropdown is accessible.

steps.content: Click the Consent Template dropdown to open it.
steps.expected: The dropdown opens showing three options: 'GDPR', 'US State Laws', and 'GDPR & US State Laws'. The 'GDPR & US State Laws' option is visually dimmed (opacity-60) and has an orange crown badge next to its label.

steps.content: Hover over the orange crown badge next to 'GDPR & US State Laws'.
steps.expected: A dark tooltip appears with the text: "Upgrade to Pro or a higher plan to unlock this feature." This is a simple informational tooltip — not the full upgrade dialog with headline, body text, and 'Upgrade now' button used for field-level premium features.

steps.content: Click the 'GDPR & US State Laws' option.
steps.expected: The template does not switch. The Consent Template dropdown closes and the active template remains unchanged (no accordion change occurs).

cases.rewrite_notes: New case — gap found during grill. The combined template uses a simple Radix tooltip on the crown badge rather than a full upgrade dialog. Requires a Basic or Free account to verify (Pro+ can select the template freely). Discovered on Basic(Trial) account in QA2 — mcp3 account (rahulkrishna+mcp3@mozilor.com) is Basic(Trial) in QA2, not Pro.

cases.title: [Cookie Banner] Verify that the 'GDPR & US State Laws' combined template shows a Customise sub-selector with GDPR and US State Laws accordion views
cases.preconditions: User is on Pro or higher plan (required to access 'GDPR & US State Laws' template). User is on Cookie Banner > Content tab.

steps.content: Open the Consent Template dropdown and click 'GDPR & US State Laws'.
steps.expected: The template switches to 'GDPR & US State Laws'. A 'Customise' sub-selector appears below the Consent Template row, defaulting to 'GDPR'. The following accordions are visible: Cookie Notice, Preference Centre, Cookie List, Revisit Consent Button, Blocked Content.

steps.content: Click the 'Customise' sub-selector and choose 'US State Laws'.
steps.expected: The accordion set switches to the US State Laws view: Cookie Notice, Opt-out Center, Cookie List, Revisit Consent Button, Blocked Content. The description text updates to reference CCPA/CPRA and related US state laws.

steps.content: Click the 'Customise' sub-selector and switch back to 'GDPR'.
steps.expected: The accordion set returns to the GDPR view: Cookie Notice, Preference Centre, Cookie List, Revisit Consent Button, Blocked Content.

cases.rewrite_notes: New case — gap found during grill. The combined template introduces a unique 'Customise' sub-selector not present in standalone GDPR or US State Laws templates. Verified on Ultimate account (jacksnickel+ultimatetest@gmail.com) in QA2. Pro plan is also expected to have access (tooltip says 'Pro or higher').
